share_basis_note: 389.19M shares outstanding x $95.96 (2026-07-10 close, Yahoo chart API S005) = ~$37.34B. Price cross-checked penny-identical vs stockanalysis (S006) per freshness.json. 52-week range $44.56-$134.92: stock is +115% off its cycle-trough low and -28.9% off its 52-week high. Diluted share count ~405M (balance-sheet-derived); market-cap identity uses 389.19M and reconciles to the reported ~$37.3B.

batch_thesis_fields.robotics_revenue_pct: ~0% (de minimis; design-wins/demos only, no dated robotics revenue/orders/units). Mix: auto ~53%, industrial+other ~32%, AI-datacenter POWER ~4% (~$500M 2026E, not robots), robotics ~0%.
batch_thesis_fields.robotics_demand_real_vs_anticipation: ANTICIPATION. Real dated cash exists for autos ($797M +5%), industrial, and AI-DC POWER ($250M'25->$500M'26E); zero dated cash for robotics.

capital_cycle.applicable: no (robotics segment) / partial (core auto-industrial + SiC + AI-DC power)
capital_cycle.stage: robotics=n/a; core DEMAND=trough->early recovery; VALUATION=late (stock +115% off low, priced-in)
capital_cycle.supply_barrier: low-med (SiC oversupply built; Sony pressuring CIS; power tree contested; only mild barrier = SiC IDM + auto qualification)
capital_cycle.read: No robotics segment for the lens to bite on. Components onsemi would sell INTO robots are commodity (4+ rivals) -> supply responds -> low durability of excess return. On the core, demand is early-cycle but the stock already re-rated, so a new buyer faces late-cycle valuation, not the trough. Robotics timing/magnitude unknown - not yet a business.
capital_cycle.caution: PEAK-lean on VALUATION (naive-guard: visible recovery after +115% = consensus, not a trough entry) -> compress verdict toward WATCH. Core demand-trough does NOT auto-raise verdict without price MOS (absent).

batch_thesis_fields.real_bottleneck_vs_concept: Robotics = CONCEPT/commodity (38.5% GM + 4 rivals + no switching cost falsifies pricing bottleneck; bottleneck node in robots is compute/actuation/software). Core auto/industrial = REAL but MED + cyclical (SiC IDM + auto design-in), not monopoly pricing.
batch_thesis_fields.value_split: $95.96 buys ~92% cyclical auto/industrial power+sensing recovery (already re-rated, no MOS) + a small real AI-DC POWER ramp + a ~$0-cash robotics/Physical-AI concept option (Synaptics narrative). Price is buying the core recovery, not a robotics bottleneck.

module_signals.finding: Q1'26 anchor fully primary (rev $1,513.3M +5%, PSG $736.6M/AMG $540.4M/ISG $236.3M, non-GAAP GM 38.5%, EPS $0.64, FCF $217.2M, Q2 guide $1,535-1,635M) + FY25 $6.0B/38.4% + balance sheet net debt -$603M + Synaptics terms. Price LIVE cross-checked penny-identical (Yahoo=stockanalysis $95.96), freshness PASS. Gaps: industrial/other exact split (O1), AI-DC $ + robotics design-win are B2-relayed mgmt (O2).

Batch thesis fails for robotics: onsemi is a cyclical auto/industrial power+sensing chipmaker inflecting off a downcycle, NOT a robotics bottleneck. Robotics revenue ~0% (anticipation); the real live mechanism is auto recovery (+5% first up in 8 qtrs) + AI-datacenter POWER ($250M->$500M). Robotics/Physical-AI is a narrative option (Synaptics).

MED, cyclical moat: auto design-in switching cost + SiC IDM (in-house substrate) are real but contested (Wolfspeed/Infineon/China SiC oversupply; Sony pressuring ~1/3 CIS share). 38.5% GM = no monopoly pricing. No CUDA-style ecosystem lock. Operator El-Khoury 3/5 (professional turnaround, margin discipline, non-founder, large dilutive Synaptics bet). Durability caps size below Core.

Real recovery with clean owner earnings: capex-light (Q1 capex 1.4% of rev), FCF $217M/qtr ~$0.9B run-rate, net debt only -$603M (survives a double-dip), ~100% of FY25 FCF to buyback + new $6B authorization. Tempered by cyclicality, a 50% GM target that is not yet earned, and ~13% all-stock dilution from Synaptics.

Not a solvency trap (net debt -$603M, no veto). The trap is valuation/timing dressed as a robotics story: a decent recovering cyclical priced as if recovery + robotics option are guaranteed, after a +115% move. Kill = GM stall <40%, SiC/CIS share loss, auto relapse, Synaptics dilution without accretion. Caps verdict at WATCH, does not veto.

At $95.96 ($37.3B) forward non-GAAP P/E ~33x, mid-cycle ~23x; base fair value ~$68 (16x $4.25 mid-cycle EPS). Even the BULL fair value ~$90 is below the current price -> NO MARGIN OF SAFETY. Stock -28.9% off its 52-week high but +115% off its low = re-rated, not a trough entry. Asymmetry negative (bull -6% / base -29% / bear -53%). PRICE + THESIS-STAGE is the binding constraint.

starter_zone: No new money at $95.96. A structured STARTER (~3-5%) opens on a pullback into ~$65-72 (base mid-cycle ~16x with a margin of safety) on an unchanged, still-recovering core thesis.
add_zone: Scale toward 8-12% (cyclical ceiling, below Core) only into ~$50-60 (toward a cycle-low retest) AND/OR on confirmation: 2H'26 gross-margin step toward mid-40s + sustained auto/industrial growth + dated AI-DC-power/robotics cash.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Current $95.96 is above even the bull fair value (~$90) -> no chase; hard no-chase toward the $134.92 52-week high. Existing holders HOLD (business unimpaired, no kill fired); do not add new money here, do not trim.
binding_constraint: PRICE + THESIS-STAGE. At $95.96 there is no margin of safety even to the bull case (~$90), and the robotics thesis that would justify a premium is ~$0 of cash (design-wins only). The core cyclical recovery is real but already re-rated +115% off its low. Completeness ~63% caps the ceiling at STARTER; price + robotics-as-option pull the applied verdict to WATCH.
kill_criteria: Non-GAAP gross-margin expansion stalls (<40% through 2H'26) or reverses.
SiC and/or CMOS-image-sensor share/ASP loss to Sony / Wolfspeed / Infineon / Chinese entrants.
Automotive/industrial demand relapses (sequential revenue declines resume) -> cyclical double-dip.
Synaptics deal: ~13% dilution without on-track $200M synergy / EPS accretion, or a value-destructive re-cut / regulatory block.
Price stays >$90-95 with no estimate upgrade -> remains WATCH / no-chase.
runner_dissent: The value instinct sees a genuine cyclical inflection with clean owner earnings (FCF ~$0.9B, net debt only -$603M), a disciplined turnaround operator, a real AI-datacenter POWER ramp, and buyback support -- and asks whether a small starter is warranted. The framework overrides to WATCH for two independent reasons: (1) PRICE -- at $95.96 there is no margin of safety even to the bull fair value (~$90); the stock has already re-rated +115% off its $44.56 low, so the cheap-cyclical entry is gone and a new buyer's asymmetry is negative. (2) THESIS-STAGE -- this is a batch about ROBOTICS bottlenecks, and onsemi's robotics revenue is ~0%; it is an auto/industrial power+sensing cyclical wearing a Physical-AI label, so it does NOT satisfy 'demand-exploded + supply-choked + already-earning.' This mirrors the PLAN.md prior: likely WATCH, and the value of the run is proving with primary data that the robotics content here is an option worth ~$0 of cash today. OPEN and left honest: exact industrial/other split (O1), A2 anchoring of AI-DC $ + robotics design-win (O2), proxy/share reconcile (O5).
